name: Georgian (Russia) Garlic Salad (Aka Marinated Garlic)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup fresh lemon juice (or balsamic vinegar)
1 tablespoon herbs (oregano is nice, or create your own mixture)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel all the garlic cloves.
2. Bring a saucepan of water to boil.
3. Once the water is boiling, turn off heat and drop the peeled garlic cloves into hot water. Let sit for 3 to 5 minutes (use a shorter time if you prefer a sharper raw garlic flavor; the longer time results in a milder garlic flavor). Remove garlic and plunge into cold water or rinse thoroughly with cold water to stop the cloves from cooking further.
4. Dissolve salt into the lemon juice.
5. Make a vinaigrette by combining olive oil, lemon juice/salt mixture, pepper, and herbs.
6. Place garlic cloves in jars and pour the oil mixture over the cloves.
7. Refrigerate for a minimum of 1 week.
After one week, eat the salad straight or slice up the garlic and mix with salad greens. Keep refrigerated. I'm not sure how long this will last in the refrigerator as I eat it up too fast.
